coreIssue: This onboarding check-in is mainly about whether Riley's fast self-serve ramp is giving him an accurate picture of team operating norms for a mid-level Backend Engineer.
openingQuestion: What parts of how this team makes backend decisions still feel easiest to infer from docs rather than from people?
listenFor: whether he names specific teammates or meetings he has used to get context, versus speaking only in docs and channels
whether he gives one concrete recent example where he asked before assuming architecture or ownership, or skips examples and stays abstract
whether he can walk through who he would contact during an incident and when he would escalate, or pauses and hedges on the sequence
avoid: do not treat fast codebase progress as proof that he understands ownership boundaries or incident expectations
do not turn the check-in into a quiz on architecture details instead of surfacing where team context is still missing
goodOutcome: You and Riley have agreed what good mid-level backend ownership looks like in his current area and one specific decision path for when to ask people before acting on architecture or incident assumptions.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one recent backend decision or incident-adjacent scenario from your team and use it to test how Riley is mapping ownership, escalation, and context sources.
